description_blurb:
This document is a mission briefing summarizing an observation of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP) by a U.S. military platform near Latakia, Syria.
A U.S. military pilot flying a P-8A aircraft reported observing an object via the aircraft’s EO/IR sensor, which they characterized as appearing to be in “sea skim mode,” traveling at approximately 500 knots (575 mph) on a southeasterly heading. The P-8A lost visual contact with the object after two minutes.
All descriptive and estimative language contained in this report reflects the reporter’s subjective interpretation at the time of the event. Such characterizations should not be interpreted as a conclusive indication of the presence or absence of any intrinsic object features or performance characteristics.
title: DOW-UAP-D55, Mission Report, Syria, November 2016
summary: This document is a mission briefing summarizing an observation of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP) by a U.S. military P-8A aircraft near Latakia, Syria. While monitoring KCTG activity in the Eastern Mediterranean, the P-8A observed an unidentified low-flying object via its EO/IR sensor. The object was characterized as appearing to be in 'sea skim mode,' traveling at approximately 500 knots (575 mph) on a southeasterly heading from an unknown origin. The P-8A lost visual contact with the object after approximately two minutes. The mission commander characterized the interaction as safe and assessed it to be standard activity consistent with the assessed activity of the KCTG.
reported_object_description: An unidentified low-flying object, characterized as a 'possible missile' or 'U/I OBJ', appearing to be in 'sea skim mode'.
reported_behavior: Traveling at approximately 500 knots (575 mph) on a southeasterly heading, outbound from KCTG, for approximately two minutes. It was observed to pass between a Russian ARS and an unidentified vessel.
official_resolution: Assessed to be standard activity consistent with the assessed activity of the KCTG.
bust_reasoning: The P-8A aircrew observed an object consistent with military activity (500 knots, sea skim mode) in an area where they were monitoring a Russian Carrier Task Group. The mission commander's assessment that it was 'standard activity consistent with the assessed activity of the KCTG' strongly points to a prosaic, albeit unidentified, Russian military craft or weapon system. There are no features that resist this explanation.
full_text:
# DOW-UAP-D55, Mission Report, Syria, November 2016 - Page 1

Declassified by MG Richard A. Harrison
USCENTCOM Chief of Staff
Declassified on: 20 Ma[redacted] 26

~~SECRET//REL TO USA, NATO~~

# 67.1 P-8A OBSERVES UNIDENTIFIED LOW-FLYING OBJECT 55 NM NORTHWEST OF LATAKIA, SYRIA

18 NOV 2016

***

**BLUF:** While monitoring KCTG activity in the Eastern Mediterranean, P-8A, observed an unidentified low-flying object 55 nm northwest of Latakia from an unknown origin, traveling at approx 500KTS on a southeasterly heading outbound from KCTG, for ~2 minutes.

**Timeline (Z):**

(1) [Redacted] 18/1310Z: P-8 observed a possible missile launch IVO [Redacted] 1.4a from an origin unknown detected via the EO/IR sensor. The possible missile appeared to be in sea skim mode traveling at approximately 500KTS on a southeasterly heading outbound from KCTG. P-8 position was 26NM S of object detection at 16121 FT [Redacted] 1.4a

(2) [Redacted] 18/1312Z: - P-8 lost visual of the object IVO [Redacted] 1.4a approx. 40 NM northwest of Latakia. The missile was observed to pass between (RUS) INGUL ARS and 1x U/I vessel.

**Weather:**
(U) P-8 aircrew characterized visibility as clear, no range limitations.

***

### FLIGHT PATH
[Image: Map showing flight path, KCTG, and various Russian vessels: (RUS) UDALOY DD-619, (RUS) KUZNETSOV CV-063 (RUS) CHILIKIN AOR, (RUS) KALININGRADNET AD, (RUS) KIROV CON 099, (RUS) INGUL ARS]

*   (1) 1310Z: [Redacted] 1.4a
*   (2) ~1312Z: [Redacted] 1.4a
*   26 NM
*   US P-8A Location 1310Z: [Redacted] 1.4a

***

[Image: Sensor footage showing "U/I OBJ", "(RUS) INGUL ARS", and "U/I VESSEL"]

**CTG 67.1 Comments:** The mission commander for P-8 characterized the interaction as safe. While this was the first observed occurrence of possible missile activity by P-8 aircraft in the Eastern Mediterranean it is assessed to be standard activity consistent with the assessed activity of the KCTG. Video footage can be found at this link: [Redacted] (b)(6)

~~SECRET//REL TO USA, NATO~~

USCENTCOM MDR 26-0038 to MDR 26-0046 | Approved for Release to AARO | 03/27/26 000001
cover_up_reasoning: The document contains routine redactions for operational security (specific locations/coordinates) and a video link, which is common for declassified military reports. It was initially classified SECRET, which is standard for such mission reports. There is no evidence of substantial concealment or suppression beyond these typical practices.
summary_zh: 本文件系一份任务简报，概述了美国军方P-8A飞机在叙利亚拉塔基亚附近对不明异常现象（UAP）的一次观测。在监测东地中海KCTG活动期间，该P-8A飞机通过其EO/IR传感器观测到一个不明低空飞行物体。该物体被描述为处于“掠海模式”，以约500节（575英里/小时）的速度沿东南方向飞行，来源不明。P-8A飞机在大约两分钟后失去了对该物体的目视接触。任务指挥官将此次互动定性为安全，并评估其为与KCTG所评估活动相符的常规活动。
reported_object_description_zh: 一个不明低空飞行物体，被描述为“可能是导弹”或“不明物体（U/I OBJ）”，似乎处于“掠海模式”。
reported_behavior_zh: 以约500节（575英里/小时）的速度沿东南方向飞行，从KCTG区域向外，持续约两分钟。据观测，其从一艘俄罗斯ARS船只和一艘不明船只之间穿过。
official_resolution_zh: 被评估为与KCTG所评估活动相符的常规活动。
bust_reasoning_zh: P-8A机组人员观测到的物体与军事活动（500节，掠海模式）相符，发生在其监测俄罗斯航母战斗群（KCTG）的区域。任务指挥官将其评估为“与KCTG所评估活动相符的常规活动”，强烈指向一个普通的、尽管身份不明的俄罗斯军事飞行器或武器系统。没有特征与此解释相悖。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 该文件包含出于行动安全（具体地点/坐标）和视频链接的常规编辑，这在解密军事报告中很常见。该文件最初被列为“秘密”，这对此类任务报告而言是标准做法。除了这些常规做法之外，没有证据表明存在实质性的隐瞒或压制。
incident_location_zh: 叙利亚拉塔基亚西北55海里
claims: A U.S. military P-8A aircraft observed an unidentified low-flying object.
The object was detected via the aircraft’s EO/IR sensor.
The object was characterized as appearing to be in 'sea skim mode'.
The object was traveling at approximately 500 knots (575 mph) on a southeasterly heading.
The observation lasted approximately two minutes.
The object was observed to pass between Russian vessels.
The interaction was assessed as safe and consistent with KCTG activity.
claims_zh: 一架美国军方P-8A飞机观测到一个不明低空飞行物体。
该物体通过飞机的EO/IR传感器被探测到。
该物体被描述为似乎处于“掠海模式”。
该物体以约500节（575英里/小时）的速度沿东南方向飞行。
观测持续了大约两分钟。
据观测，该物体从俄罗斯船只之间穿过。
此次互动被评估为安全且与KCTG活动相符。
bust_explanations: Russian military missile: The object's description as a 'possible missile' in 'sea skim mode' traveling at 500 knots, observed while monitoring a Russian Carrier Task Group (KCTG), strongly suggests a Russian military missile launch or test. The mission commander's assessment of 'standard activity consistent with the assessed activity of the KCTG' supports this.

Russian military drone/UAV: A high-speed military drone or Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) operating in a 'sea skim' profile could also match the observed kinematics and context of monitoring Russian naval activity.

Low-flying Russian military aircraft: While 'sea skim mode' typically implies very low altitude, a low-flying Russian military jet or other aircraft could be initially misidentified as a missile or unidentified object, especially when observed via EO/IR sensors from a distance.
bust_explanations_zh: 俄罗斯军用导弹：该物体被描述为“可能是导弹”，处于“掠海模式”，以500节的速度飞行，且在监测俄罗斯航母战斗群（KCTG）时被观测到，这强烈表明是一次俄罗斯军用导弹发射或测试。任务指挥官将其评估为“与KCTG所评估活动相符的常规活动”支持了这一解释。
俄罗斯军用无人机/UAV：一架高速军用无人机或UAV（Unmanned Aerial Vehicle）以“掠海”姿态飞行，也可能与观测到的运动学特征以及监测俄罗斯海军活动的背景相符。
俄罗斯低空军用飞机：尽管“掠海模式”通常意味着极低的高度，但一架低空飞行的俄罗斯军用喷气式飞机或其他飞机，尤其是在通过EO/IR传感器从远处观测时，最初可能被误识别为导弹或不明物体。
cover_up_indicators: Initial classification as 'SECRET//REL TO USA, NATO'
cover_up_indicators_zh: 具体坐标/地点被编辑（[已编辑] 1.4a）
视频片段链接被编辑（[已编辑] (b)(6)）
最初分类为“秘密//限美国、北约共享”
